_meta.generatedFrom: m3-knowledge-base (layer-0 business PRD/SOW = contract scope; layer-1 product = team working scope)
_meta.lifecycleNote: All features are lifecycleState=draft: KB provides scope, not the feature-by-feature hour breakdown (that is produced at the BET-lock exercise). Estimates are intentionally null.
notes: ALF (Unified Intelligence Layer) is modeled as a Build — it is tracked as Phase 2 (Platform Fabric) in the project tracker. Phase 1 Structured Discovery (~1650h) and Phase 5 ongoing retainer are not seeded as builds.

Estimate provenance: the per-phase hour totals (VetLeo 3333, Raivan 10000, ALF 5000) are the SALES-PROCESS feasibility estimates — i.e., the Draft/Tollgate-1 numbers. That estimation step happened outside the system; here they seed each build's budget envelope.
Reconciliation examples are included: one unplanned contract-scope item (DSO/multi-location) and team-added features (e.g., social bill-splitting) with empty contractScopeRefs.

contract.name: M3 Unified Veterinary Intelligence Platform — SOW (Phases 1–5)
contract.changeOrderPolicy: Changes require a signed Change Order; client has 10 business days to approve/reject. Backlog re-prioritization does not require a Change Order unless it materially alters product vision or team capacity.

_briefNote: Non-sensitive scaffolding only. dbdos.json is committed to a PUBLIC repo; the actual client narrative (founder background, financials, working-style sensitivities, stakeholder detail) stays in the PRIVATE knowledge base and is pulled live via /api/kb, gated by the access secret. Sections tagged sensitivity:full render leadership-only; kbRefs are pointers, not content.

brief.headline: Dualboot × M3 — building a unified veterinary intelligence platform: Raivan (clinical OS), VetLeo (consumer companion), and ALF (shared data fabric).

sections.title: Project Background
sections.intro: How this engagement came to Dualboot, the strategic rationale behind it, and the organizational goals and constraints that frame delivery. The full narrative is maintained in the Knowledge Base (Layer 0 · Business).

sections.title: Buyer Background
sections.intro: Who the buyer is — company stage, founder background, decision-making context, and the working-style sensitivities the delivery team should understand before engaging.

sections.title: Stakeholder Mapping
sections.intro: The people and decision structure on the client side — who decides what, approval paths, and the key external relationships in play.

sections.title: Product Vision
sections.intro: What M3 is building and the role each product plays in the ecosystem. The cards below reflect the current product registry; the deeper vision narrative lives in the Knowledge Base.

sections.title: How Concrete Is the Idea?
sections.intro: A read on how firm each product's definition is today — derived from feature-level confidence — surfacing where the idea is settled versus still emerging.

sections.title: Look & Feel · Design Assets
sections.intro: The approved creative direction and where design assets live.
highlights: Brand ownership transferred to Dualboot's design team (2026-07-07 branding sync); the external brand partner is now review-only.
AI persona voice — Raivan/FLO: competent, direct, trustworthy (Admiral Janeway). Leo (VetLeo): relentlessly positive + puzzle-solving + reassuring.
Leo mascot uses a flat / 2D line-art illustration style (not 3D) — chosen for animation, readability, and an approachable tone.
Approach is visual-from-day-one: directional wireframes early, with final branding dropping in progressively.

sections.title: Feature Definitions & Certainty
sections.intro: The scoping team's read on each feature: confidence level, open unknowns, delivery risk, and how it maps to the contract (aligned / reinterpreted / team-added).

sections.title: Scope Reconciliation
sections.intro: Where the client's stated (contract) scope and the team's working plan agree — and where they don't.

sections.title: Open Questions & Gaps
sections.intro: Known gaps and unresolved questions from scoping, plus the feature-level unknowns the team has flagged — surfaced so nothing hides.

sections.title: Supporting Documentation
sections.intro: The source material behind this brief. Full text lives in the private Knowledge Base.

openQuestions.text: Raivan scope is under active reassessment — a dedicated Raivan scoping session was requested but not yet held; treat its documented scope as provisional until it resolves.
VetLeo AI-triage safety / liability boundary is not yet defined — a blocking artifact for the triage feature.
VetLeo go-to-market staging (geographic / metro rollout) remains open — to confirm during Discovery.
Stakeholder register not yet validated by the Product Director; several client-side roles remain to be confirmed.
Several constraints (data-residency, compliance certifications, AI human-review boundaries) are flagged 'needs clarification' and unresolved.

entryCriteria: Scope reconciliation reviewed (no unresolved unplanned/team-added gaps)

builds.name: VetLeo
builds.description: Consumer mobile app — a lifecycle pet companion; the first major signal generator feeding the intelligence layer. Conversational-first, species-agnostic from day one.

builds.name: Raivan
builds.description: Signal-driven veterinary operating system / practice management system at parity with modern PIMS, adding orchestration so one clinical action cascades across records, billing, inventory, scheduling, and comms.

builds.name: ALF — Unified Intelligence Layer
builds.description: Platform fabric: shared data + intelligence foundation that Raivan and VetLeo both feed and consume — core signal architecture, multi-tenancy framework, foundational data models. Tracked as Phase 2 (Platform Fabric).

contractScope.name: VetLeo MVP mobile app
contractScope.description: Deliver an initial VetLeo mobile application as a lifecycle pet companion; first signal generator for the intelligence layer. Per SOW, specific capabilities to be defined in Discovery (deliberately open at contract time).

contractScope.name: PIMS functional parity
contractScope.description: Full parity with modern cloud PIMS: scheduling, clinical SOAP documentation, billing/invoicing, inventory, client communications, reporting, prescriptions & labs, secure multi-device (incl. mobile) access.

contractScope.name: Orchestration differentiators
contractScope.description: Voice-to-SOAP, voice-to-task automation, e-ink cage cards, referral portal with two-way case communication, staff scheduling integrated with clinical capacity.

contractScope.name: Required MVP integrations
contractScope.description: IDEXX, Antech, Zoetis, Heska (labs); IDEXX WebPACS, Sound (imaging); CareCredit, Scratchpay (financing); Trupanion; QuickBooks, Xero; Gusto/ADP/Paychex/Rippling (payroll); Cubex (controlled substances).

contractScope.name: DSO / multi-location enterprise features
contractScope.description: Corporate group accounts, multi-site consolidated dashboards, DSO contracting/onboarding tooling. Client may expect at platform level; team has DEFERRED full support to post-MVP.

contractScope.name: Platform fabric foundation
contractScope.description: Core signal architecture, multi-tenancy framework, and foundational data models supporting both clinical and consumer workflows. Specific outcomes to be defined in Discovery.

features.name: Getting started (onboarding)
features.description: Account creation, add pet(s) (any species), minimal health baseline, sharing choices. First meaningful Leo moment in under 5 minutes.

mapping.ticketConvention: ProjectX entry lists ticket# → GitHub issue → this feature

features.name: My pet(s) & their history
features.description: One trustworthy home for who the pet is + document uploads (vaccine certs, records); multi-pet from day one; the health timeline starts here.

features.name: Everyday care (daily habit loop)
features.description: Owner logs (or Leo prompts) daily care — walks, feeding, litter, sleep, grooming — plus a quick visual check; Leo notices patterns and speaks up. Highest retention mechanism in the quiet years.

unknowns: how to make active logging effortless without device integration
value-exchange feedback loop (data in → insight out)

features.name: When something seems wrong (triage with Leo)
features.description: Full triage conversation with Leo incl. photo/video + complete escalation path (watch → monitor → call your vet → go now → emergency). The differentiator; ships at launch.

unknowns: escalation-states map not yet created (blocking artifact)

features.name: The vet visit (capture + plain-language summary)
features.description: Prep before the visit, capture the recommendation (photo/recording), plain-language summary the owner keeps. Excludes appointment scheduling and Raivan integration at launch.

unknowns: recording capture UX in a live exam

features.name: Post-visit follow-through
features.description: Today's meds, today's instructions, a 'did you give this?' check-in, and a simple sense of recovery. Excludes long-term/ongoing medication management (later phase).

unknowns: boundary between acute follow-through and sustained med management

features.name: Leo (conversational assistant — cross-cutting 'Power')
features.description: Leo is not a screen — it is woven across every Place: proactive prompts, pattern-noticing, plain-language help. Built as behaviors/components, not a tab.

unknowns: how 'Power' work is estimated vs 'Place' work

features.name: EMR + SOAP clinical documentation
features.description: Central clinical record: SOAP notes, problem lists, diagnoses, treatment history, imaging attachments; receives external lab results; destination for the voice-to-task pipeline.

unknowns: parity checklist vs incumbent PIMS not finalized

features.name: Patient scheduling
features.description: Appointment booking (self-service mobile/web, phone-agent, front-desk), real-time availability, waitlist management.

features.name: Staff scheduling (capacity-aware)
features.description: Shift assignment, coverage checks against clinical capacity (e.g., surgeries booked vs qualified scrub nurses), clock-in/out analytics.

features.name: Front desk & patient flow (CSR) + AI-assisted CSR
features.description: Check-in (mobile-first), waiting room, room assignment, checkout, payment collection; near-self-service kiosk flow; live-call transcription feeding an additive context stream to the human CSR.

unknowns: kiosk vs staffed flow scope split

features.name: Voice-to-task pipeline
features.description: The defining differentiator: converts exam/surgery speech into structured SOAP entries, nursing tasks, invoice line items, and inventory updates. Requires dedicated room audio + explicit manual activation.

unknowns: voice-to-SOAP viability in real exam-room acoustics (validate at The Sams Clinic)
one of the platform-bootstrapping capabilities (effort includes standing up plumbing)

features.name: Admitted patient task sheet + digital cage cards
features.description: Time-sequenced task sheet for admitted patients; networked e-ink cage-card displays showing the current sheet, with QR to the full record.

features.name: Billing / closeout
features.description: Invoice generation from accumulated line items; multi-payer split; third-party payment facilitation. Payment processing is a Raivan revenue source.

features.name: Social bill-splitting + BNPL (emergency/high-cost)
features.description: Shareable GoFundMe-style contribution link at checkout, structured multi-payer split, instant buy-now-pay-later pre-qualification. Doubles as brand exposure to the owner's network.

unknowns: team-originated concept — confirm client wants it in MVP vs later

features.name: FLO launch trust-set (AI)
features.description: Trust-first AI launch set: voice-to-SOAP (1.1), allergy-conflict blocking (4.8), abnormal-lab flagging (1.4). Chosen for natural precision / asked-for relief; no historical-data dependency.

unknowns: FLO platform (D1) plumbing built alongside first capabilities

features.name: Core signal architecture
features.description: The shared event/signal backbone both Raivan and VetLeo feed and consume. Foundational; architecture validated in Discovery.

unknowns: architecture validated in Discovery; foundational decisions lock Phase 1

features.name: Multi-tenancy framework
features.description: Tenancy + isolation model spanning clinical (Raivan) and consumer (VetLeo) workflows.
